explanation: This command uses an NTFS short name (e.g., 'Progra~1') to reference a directory, which would trigger the Sigma rule. It simulates a scenario where an attacker might use short names to evade detection.

This command uses a short name (e.g., 'Docume~1') to reference a user directory and executes a PowerShell script. It demonstrates how short names can be used in command lines to bypass detection mechanisms.

This command directly uses an executable with a short name (e.g., 'MyShor~1.EXE'), which matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria. It represents a typical evasion technique using 8.3 filename notation.
